summary: Created MPI-169 — Settings disk-usage progress bar for the selected DC's network volume (used/total GB). Investigated read-only: total=vol.size (already shown in badge), MpiProgressBar primitive exists to reuse, mount slot = _renderRunpodVolume in MpiSettings.js. BLOCKER: used-bytes not exposed by RunPod REST/GraphQL nor /wrapper/stats (statvfs disk block was reverted MPI-100 — reads container overlay not the volume quota). Numerator only measurable via du inside a running Pod. Card: Stage-1 spike an undocumented RunPod used-bytes GraphQL field (introspection off, probe live); else wrapper path (add GET /wrapper/disk du -sb /workspace, works only while CONNECTED to that DC's Pod). Open product decision logged: does 'selected DC' need to work when NOT connected?

SPIKE DONE (live API probe, research/api-probe-2026-07-01.md): RunPod exposes NO volume used-bytes. REST /networkvolumes = {id,name,size,dc} only; GraphQL NetworkVolume type REJECTS used/usedBytes/consumedBytes/currentPerGBUsage. CONFIRMED DEAD — no API path, no zero-pod option. Bar is CONNECTED-POD-ONLY via a new wrapper GET /wrapper/disk (du -sb /workspace). Best fit = CPU download Pod. Product decision settled (was open): 'selected DC when not connected' is impossible; idle DC keeps total-only badge. Denominator vol.size already in the badge; MpiProgressBar reused.

Scope widened (user): bar must work on BOTH a GPU pod AND a CPU download pod, not CPU-only — both mount the volume at /workspace so `du /workspace` is engine-agnostic; wrapper endpoint lives in the shared wrapper, app-side gate is 'a pod is connected' not pod type. Connected-pod-only constraint ACCEPTED by user. CPU download pod remains the best demo (fullness matters most there) but not the limit.

BUILT: wrapper GET /wrapper/disk (du -sb /workspace) v0.2.22→0.2.23 in mpi-ci; app /remote/pod/disk route (wrapper-first, no REST fallback) + WRAPPER_VERSION pin 0.2.20→0.2.23; MpiProgressBar in _renderRunpodVolume (hidden until a pod reports, 10s poll, danger ≥90%, teardown wired); CSS. Works GPU pod AND CPU pod (both mount /workspace). Syntax+ESLint clean, route live, app boots clean. BLOCKER to SEE the bar: publish wrapper 0.2.23 to R2 (publish-runtime.sh, no rebuild, LIVE stable-channel op = user-gated). Degrades gracefully until then (old wrapper 404 → bar hidden). validation.md has the test script.

R2 PUBLISH DONE: wrapper 0.2.23 pushed to cubric-pod-runtime/vision/stable (publish-runtime.sh, no rebuild). Verified public: manifest wrapper_version=0.2.23, wrapper.py 80KB contains /wrapper/disk, version line reads 0.2.23. Blocker cleared — a fresh pod boot (or POST /wrapper/restart-comfy) now serves /wrapper/disk. Disk bar ready for live test on GPU or CPU pod. (publish curl -w '%{http_code}' printed 000 in-sandbox but content fetches return real data — cosmetic sandbox quirk, files ARE served.)

doing→done, complete/accepted. USER-VERIFIED live: inline disk bar shows 65GB / 130GB on a connected pod. wrapper 0.2.23 published to R2 stable + committed mpi-ci a5d1812; app committed 2b3f182 (RunPod branch). Works GPU+CPU pods, connected-pod-only by design.
